title: add sync-production skill
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds `.claude/skills/sync-production/` for merging `main` into `production` via the GitHub merges API — no local checkout, no working tree changes.

Pre-checks for conflicts with `git merge-tree` before calling the API. If the merge would conflict, the script lists the conflicting paths and exits non-zero so the agent can surface the reason in chat instead of forcing it through.

Run with:
```
.claude/skills/sync-production/scripts/sync-production.sh
```

Exit codes: `0` success/no-op, `2` would-conflict, `3` API rejected.

gist.headline: Claude learns to ship
gist.blurb: We taught our AI agents a new trick: merging main to production directly via the GitHub API. No local checkouts, no messy working trees, just clean deployments.
gist.summary: Added a new Claude skill that merges the main branch into production using the GitHub merges API, including pre-checks for conflicts via git merge-tree.
gist.impact: Agents can now safely sync production branches without altering local working trees, surfacing clear error codes if merge conflicts are detected.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny construction hard hat, working alongside the monitor robot at a retro terminal. They are pressing a glowing 'MERGE' button to ship code, representing the automated GitHub API merge process.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny construction hard hat, hovering next to a chunky monitor robot at a retro terminal. The robot is pressing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) 'MERGE' button. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
